entries.title: Corellian Crisis Centers Local Autonomy Pressure

content.fact: The Corellian crisis tests New Republic unity through local autonomy, Corellian identity, and Centerpoint Station stakes.
content.injection: During Corellian Crisis context, emphasize internal Republic fracture and local politics more than Imperial invasion.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Corellian Crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Corellian Crisis to Corellian Crisis.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

continuity.sourceBoundary: Legends post-Endor New Republic material from the immediate aftermath of Return of the Jedi through the Hand of Thrawn peace settlement, before the New Jedi Order era begins.
continuity.notes: Development deck. Keep separate from Disney canon post-Endor and sequel-era continuity.

entries.title: Centerpoint Is Not a Generic Station

content.fact: Centerpoint Station carries ancient and dangerous system-scale capabilities, not ordinary orbital infrastructure.
content.injection: In Corellian Crisis, only use Centerpoint as a massive ancient strategic object when Corellian Crisis context or explicit premise supports it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Corellian Crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Corellian Crisis to Corellian Crisis.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Black Fleet Crisis Has Military and Political Separation

content.fact: The Black Fleet Crisis combines external military threat, New Republic politics, and personal search threads without being an Imperial Remnant campaign.
content.injection: If using Black Fleet context, do not default the enemy to Imperial Remnant; keep the Yevetha-specific conflict distinct.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Black Fleet Crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Black Fleet Crisis to Black Fleet Crisis.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Mid-Era Threats Are Not All Sith

context.label: Mid New Republic crises
context.phase: Mid New Republic to Hand of Thrawn Era

content.fact: Many mid-New Republic threats are political, military, local, or alien-state conflicts rather than Sith plots.
content.injection: Do not force every New Republic crisis into Jedi/Sith structure; use the appropriate faction and pressure type. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Mid New Republic crises.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
